calendar.matters:
ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar Feb-06-2026 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-08-2024 Off Calendar

Notice Of Application For Default Judgment By Court
Off Calendar Jun-26-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar for a request for default judgment against Defendant John David Hamner and Defendant Dataspace Advisors, Incorporated.

Dept.505; Judge: Anne Costin; Clerk: K. Valdes; Proceedings reported by "For the Record" only to be used in compliance with California Government Code Section 69957, commencing at 10:04:09 a.m. pausing at 10:29:38 a.m., resuming at 10:45:55a.m. and concluding at 11:07:22 a.m. (D505)

Nina Montoya, Esq. representing Plaintiff Heather Craig via Zoom Video Conference.
Plaintiff Heather Craig was placed under oath, and testified via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant John David Hamner and Defendant Dataspace Advisors, Incorporated not appearing.

Plaintiff's 1st amended complaint for Sexual Harassment, Discrimination, Breach of contract, et al filed on Feb-17-2023.
Proof of substituted service as to Defendant John David Hamner and Defendant Dataspace Advisors, Incorporated on Apr-17-2023; service effectuated on Apr-29-2023.
Default entered as to Defendant John David Hamner and Defendant Dataspace Advisors, Incorporated on Jun-08-2023.
Dismissal filed against Defendants Does 1 through 10, inclusive on Apr-11-2024.

The Court inquires as to the untimely filing of default against defendants. Counsel to provide proof as to the Court's inquiry.
The Court finds good cause to move forward with the hearing excusing the untimely filing of the default against the defendants.
Counsel to provide proof as to the causes of actions.
Plaintiff is called to testify. Plaintiff confirms counsel's statements are true and correct.
The Court examines Plaintiff. Plaintiff testifies.
Counsel to offer proof as to relief sought in the prayer of the complaint.
Court takes a 15-minute recess.
Proceedings are back on the record.
Plaintiff Craig testifies as to the relief sought for emotional distress.
Counsel examines Plaintiff.
Plaintiff Craig testifies as to Defendant Hamner's personal financial capacity.
Counsel provides proof as to the attorney's fees request.

After hearing the testimony, reviewing the declaration, and considering the evidence, the Court does not find that the damages are appropriate to the evidence presented.

The Court grants judgment in favor of the Plaintiff Heather Craig against the Defendant John David Hamner and Defendant Dataspace Advisors, Incorporated as follows:

Past Wages -Damages $103,143.60
Emotional Distress $100,000.00
Penalties in violation of Labor Code 1194 and 1197 $2,400.00
Penalties in violation of Labor Code 204 $800.00
Penalties in violation of Labor Code 1174 $500.00
Penalties in violation of Labor Code 201, 202, and 203 $4,800.00
Attorney's Fees $68,175.00
Costs $2,210.97

Total Judgment $282,029.57

Proposed judgment to submitted to the court for review and approval.
